summary: Patrick McKenzie posts a brief holiday greeting to fellow micro-ISV developers, noting his travel from Japan to Chicago for family time. He teases upcoming announcements for early January including a Google partnership, the launch of Bingo Card Creator 2.0, and a year-end review. The post also doubles as an SEO play, with a postscript directly targeting Google searchers looking for Christmas and New Year's bingo cards, linking them to his free offerings.
key_points: McKenzie uses even a casual holiday greeting post as an opportunity for SEO by targeting seasonal search queries for Christmas and New Year's bingo cards
He teases multiple business milestones for early 2008: a Google announcement, BCC 2.0 launch, and site redesigns
The post illustrates how consistent content creation and strategic keyword targeting work even in low-effort blog posts
money_quotes: See you in early January for my announcement with Google, the official launch of Bingo Card Creator 2.0, the year-end wrapup for 2007, and (possibly, depending on the amount of time I have) a redesign of the Bingo Card Creator and Daily Bingo Cards web sites.
Hiya, Google searchers : You're probably looking for Christmas bingo cards or maybe New Years bingo cards.
Yep, I've got them and yep, they're free — just click the underlined blue text.
Feel free to pass those links around.
key_insight: Every blog post is a marketing opportunity — even a holiday greeting can be optimized to capture seasonal search traffic and drive conversions.
